textDisplay: So, this trailer's song is Strangers, which is about a fictional character, Ethel Cain, who is the central figure of the album. Each song is a chapter in that fictional character's story, with Strangers being a truly unique song, posited as a 'song' or a final goodbye from the Ethel Cain character to her mother after Ethel was killed in the storyline and eaten by her lover. Took me awhile to realize all this.....I really don't understand why everyone below is so up in arms....it's a song, and most likely apropos for the movie.

textDisplay: Idk why people are complaining about the song choice without understanding the context of why they chose it and why Ethel Cain’s story works. The song actually fits the plot of this movie (which is based on the book) pretty well. The cheater Lily vowed not to be in an abusive relationship like her mother and father yet falls in love with a man who ends up abusing her and you see both the humanity and horror of the love story… it also lends an empathetic lens to women who find it hard to leave abusive relationships because they are often in love with the one that hurts them. It’s called It Ends With Us because it is a choice to end the cycle of abuse, before the abuser ends your livelihood.
textOriginal: Idk why people are complaining about the song choice without understanding the context of why they chose it and why Ethel Cain’s story works. The song actually fits the plot of this movie (which is based on the book) pretty well. The cheater Lily vowed not to be in an abusive relationship like her mother and father yet falls in love with a man who ends up abusing her and you see both the humanity and horror of the love story… it also lends an empathetic lens to women who find it hard to leave abusive relationships because they are often in love with the one that hurts them. It’s called It Ends With Us because it is a choice to end the cycle of abuse, before the abuser ends your livelihood.
